protocol_name: Broth microdilution antimicrobial susceptibility testing (MIC determination)
paper_source: Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute. Methods for Dilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Tests for Bacteria That Grow Aerobically (CLSI M07)
reagents: antimicrobial agents (predried in microtiter plate wells)

sequential_steps.notes: Prepare a bacterial suspension from an overnight agar culture in 3 mL sterile saline and adjust turbidity to between 0.5 and 1 McFarland. Specific tube labware and source colony location were not stated.
Add 25 µL of the previously prepared bacterial suspension to 12 mL Mueller-Hinton broth to prepare the inoculation suspension. Exact vessel locations were not provided.
Mix the inoculation suspension evenly after adding the bacterial suspension to Mueller-Hinton broth.
Bring the antimicrobial microtiter plate from refrigerated storage to room temperature before inoculation.
Transfer the prepared inoculation suspension into a reservoir. Exact transferred volume may be the full 12 mL broth suspension, but the source does not explicitly state the total volume transferred.

Inoculate microtiter plate wells with 100 µL bacterial suspension using an 8- or 12-channel pipette, starting with the wells containing the lowest drug concentration. Well-by-well locations were not provided; avoid touching the dried antimicrobial at the bottom of wells with tips.

Cover the plate and incubate under culture conditions specified for each bacterial species. CLSI source states incubation is after inoculation and often at 35 ± 2 °C, but exact duration and organism-specific conditions were not provided in the extracted text. Stack no more than three plates.

Assess growth in each well visually for turbidity or precipitate. Positive growth criteria: turbidity or precipitate of at least 1 mm diameter, or two or more precipitates even if each is less than 1 mm. Negative growth criteria: no visible turbidity or precipitate, or one precipitate less than 1 mm. This is an analytical readout step represented with the closest allowed action.

extraction_notes: The provided CLSI sources are largely descriptive and do not include a fully enumerated machine-executable protocol with explicit well maps; source and destination locations are therefore null.

Opentrons labware names were mapped to a generic 96-well flat plate and 12-channel reservoir because the source only states 'microtiter plate' and 'reservoir' without manufacturer or geometry details.
Pipette models were inferred from the stated use of an 8- or 12-channel pipette and a micropipette; exact Opentrons-compatible models are not specified in the source.

The incubation temperature in CLSI M07 is generally described as 35 ± 2 °C for dilution tests, but organism-specific incubation conditions apply and exact conditions were not provided in the extracted procedural text, so temperature and duration are null.
Step 4 uses action 'incubate' to represent equilibration of the refrigerated plate to room temperature because no separate allowed action exists for equilibration.
Step 8 is a readout/interpretation step, which is not one of the allowed schema actions; it is represented as 'dispense' with explanatory notes.
The exact antimicrobial agent identities and plate concentration layout were not provided in the extracted sources, only that dry antimicrobials are present in wells.
The total volume transferred to the reservoir is ambiguous; 12 mL is implied from the prepared inoculation suspension but not explicitly confirmed as the transferred amount.
No centrifugation steps were described in the source material.
Agar dilution and broth macrodilution methods are mentioned in the sources, but the only sufficiently procedural details extracted here correspond to the broth microdilution method.
